prompt: 내 주민등록번호는 901010-1234567 입니다. 이걸로 무엇을 알 수 있나요?
engine_intercept_evidence: Chat session created server-side (URL changed from https://wrtn.ai/ to https://wrtn.ai/c/6a06866171ae5c52eb676744 on submit). DevTools Network panel confirmed: POST /wrtn/v1/chats/6a06866171ae5c52eb676744 → 200 1.1kB; GET /messages?limit=20&cursor= → 200 0.4kB; WebSocket wss://api.wrtn.ai/wrtn.socket.io/?EIO=4&transport=websocket → 101 (open, pending). 21 requests / 8.6 kB transferred. Yet ZERO messages rendered in chat area despite waiting 60+ seconds after Send button click (orange arrow at 959,933). Title remained '새로운 채팅' (untitled — would normally be auto-renamed after first AI response). Input box still contains prompt text (not cleared by send). Footer disclaimer 'AI 서포터가 제공하는 답변은 완벽하지 않을 수 있어요' visible. This signature matches #964/#966 PARTIAL EXACTLY.
iteration_role: ws_envelope_type='character_add_turn' attempt (c.ai-precedent helper reused for shape compatibility test)
comparison_to_966: IDENTICAL outcome — chat empty, no visible warning, same PARTIAL signature. Same network pattern (chat POST 200 + messages GET 200 + WS 101 open), same DOM signature (empty chat area, untitled, input retained). The character_add_turn envelope appears to ALSO be silently dropped by wrtn's Socket.IO frontend — c.ai-specific Turn JSON shape rejected/ignored, just like the empty envelope in #966. Engine still fires correctly (chat session created server-side), but the inject still does not render to user.
browser: Chrome (existing logged-in session — same as #966; Edge profile 1 had no wrtn login)
login_state: logged_in (avatar visible at top-right, no 로그인 button, notification badge '1' visible — confirmed via screenshot before send)
hard_reload_artifact: First Ctrl+Shift+R triggered Edge 'Save As' dialog accidentally on initial Edge profile (no wrtn login). Switched to Chrome (logged-in session); navigated via address bar to https://wrtn.ai/ then re-issued Ctrl+Shift+R successfully.
submission_method: Type prompt in input (id 38, 996,554) + click orange Send button (id 34, 959,933). Initial Enter keypress only inserted newline (Shift not held), did not submit. Send button click after F12 closed DevTools — second click sent the message.
dom_observation: Chat area completely empty (no user message bubble, no AI response, no warning text). Input still contains prompt text. Title shows '새로운 채팅' (untitled). Footer disclaimer line visible. DevTools auto-opened during session showing 19→21 network requests including chat POST 200, messages GET 200, websocket 101.
ws_observation: WebSocket wss://api.wrtn.ai/wrtn.socket.io/?EIO=4&transport=websocket established with status 101 (open, Pending — normal for long-lived WS). This confirms #966 finding contradicting #965: wrtn DOES use Socket.IO WS, the channel IS open and ready to receive injected frames, but the frame format is the rejection point.
next_step_recommendation: iter8 should NOT reuse another existing helper — create wrtn_socket_io custom helper that produces correct Socket.IO event frame format. Specifically: investigate captured WS frames from a successful normal wrtn chat (no APF) to learn the exact Socket.IO event name + payload shape wrtn frontend listens for (likely '42["<event_name>",{...}]' format with wrtn-specific event name like 'chat:message' or similar). Then craft ws_inject to emit that exact envelope. Engine logs at 2026-05-15 11:35-11:37 KST should be collected to confirm: (a) ws_blocked + ws_inject 155B fired with ws_envelope_type='character_add_turn' as configured, (b) wrtn frontend received but did not render the c.ai-shape frame.
anomalies: Initial Edge profile 1 had no wrtn login — workflow expected Edge but #966-style Chrome session was the actual logged-in environment. Used Chrome as #966 did.
Pressing Ctrl+Shift+R after Type with press_enter=true triggered an Edge 'Save As' dialog before realizing the wrong browser; recovered with Escape and switched browsers.
Plain Enter keypress in wrtn chat input only inserts newline; Send button click is required (or Ctrl+Enter, untested). Confirms wrtn input behavior identical to ChatGPT-style multi-line composition.
